title: Reading Comprehension Questions
tagline: Cite Evidence | Support Inferences | Analyze Details
objectives:
• Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly
• Make inferences from informational text using supporting evidence
directions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer for questions 1-5. Write your response for questions 6-10.

questions.question: Which evidence best supports the author's main claim?
questions.answer: C - A quote from an expert

questions.question: What can you infer about the topic from paragraph 3?

options.C: It is only for young people

questions.question: The phrase 'on the other hand' in paragraph 5 suggests:
Which detail from the text supports the conclusion in paragraph 6?
What does the author explicitly state about the main topic?
Cite one piece of evidence that supports the author's argument and explain how it relates to the main claim.
questions.answer: Students should identify specific textual evidence and explain its connection to the author's main argument, demonstrating understanding of how evidence supports claims in informational texts.

questions.question: What inference can you make about the author's purpose? Support your answer with evidence from the text.
questions.answer: Students should make a reasonable inference about why the author wrote the text and cite specific details or language choices that support their inference about the author's intent.

questions.question: Identify an implicit message in the text and explain what evidence led you to this understanding.
questions.answer: Students should recognize information that is suggested but not directly stated, then cite specific textual clues that helped them understand this implicit meaning.

questions.question: Analyze how the author uses evidence throughout the text to build credibility. Cite specific examples in your response.
questions.answer: Students should examine multiple pieces of evidence the author presents, explain how each type of evidence strengthens the author's credibility, and demonstrate understanding of how authors build trustworthiness through various forms of support.

questions.question: Evaluate the strength of the author's overall argument by examining the quality and relevance of the evidence provided.
questions.answer: Students should assess whether the evidence effectively supports the main argument, considering factors like source credibility, relevance to claims, and sufficiency of support while citing specific examples from the text.
